title: Remember + -ing (the past)
body: Here, **remember** is followed by **-ing** because you recall something you did before (**remember doing** it). With **to + verb**, **remember** means you must not forget to do something later.

title: Enjoy + -ing
body: **Enjoy** is usually followed by **-ing**, not **to + verb**.

title: Decide + to + verb
body: **Decide** is followed by **to + verb** when you talk about a choice about a future action.

title: Stop + to + verb (purpose)
body: **Stopped to eat** means we stopped so we could eat. **Stop + -ing** means we quit an activity. After **stop**, **to + verb** can show **why** you stopped.

title: Forget + to + verb
body: **Don't forget to practice** means something you still need to do. **Forget + -ing** means you forgot something that already happened.
hint: Future task not done → forget + to + verb.

title: Stop: two different forms
body: **Stop** can use both **-ing** and **to + verb**. The meaning depends on which form you use.
hint: This verb can use both forms; the meaning changes.

title: Correct
body: Read the rule below to remember it.

title: Incorrect
body: Learn the rule below.

title: Phrases like interested in, good at + -ing
body: Phrases like **interested in**, **good at**, **tired of** are fixed. The next verb is usually **-ing**, not **to + verb**.
hint: After this kind of phrase, use -ing.

title: After / before / without + -ing
body: **After**, **before**, and **without** can introduce an action with **-ing** when we already know who did it (**After eating** can mean **after we ate**).

title: Finish + -ing
body: **Finish** is followed by **-ing** when you talk about completing an activity (**finish doing** something).

title: Avoid + -ing
body: **Avoid** is usually followed by **-ing**, not **to + verb**.

title: Want + to + verb
body: **Want** is followed by **to + verb** when you talk about something you would like to do.

title: Hope + to + verb
body: **Hope** is often followed by **to + verb** when you talk about a future wish.

title: Agree + to + verb
body: **Agree** is followed by **to + verb** when people decide to do something together.

title: Both forms: same meaning
body: This verb often works with **-ing** **or** **to + verb**. The idea stays **similar**. It is **not** the same as **Tricky verbs: different meaning**.
hint: -ing or to + verb — both can be OK here.

title: Verb + to + verb
body: This verb is usually followed by **to + verb** in this lesson.
hint: Use to + base verb after this verb.

title: Verb + -ing
body: This verb is usually followed by **-ing** in this lesson.
hint: Use the -ing form after this verb.

title: Try: two different ideas
body: **Try + -ing** can mean *test something*. **Try + to + verb** often means *make an effort*.
hint: Test or effort? -ing vs to + verb.

title: Regret: past vs formal news
body: **Regret + -ing** is about the **past**. **Regret + to + verb** is often formal bad news (**regret to inform**).
hint: Past mistake → -ing. Formal message → to + verb.

title: Mean: intend vs imply
body: **Mean + to + verb** = intend. **Mean + -ing** (or clause) can express what something **involves**.
hint: Intend → to + verb. Involve / imply → often -ing.

title: Go on: continue vs next topic
body: **Go on + -ing** = continue the same activity. **Go on + to + verb** can mean continue **with a new action**.
hint: Same activity → -ing. Next step → to + verb.

title: Remember + to + verb
body: **Remember + to + verb** means *don't forget to do something later* (a future task).
hint: Reminder for later → remember + to + verb.

title: Forget + -ing (the past)
body: **Forget + -ing** can mean you forgot something that **already happened**.

title: Stop + -ing
body: **Stop + -ing** means you **quit** an activity (**stop talking** = dejar de hablar).
